rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LI VS. MEI LING FANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Set Aside Default
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 16, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANT SHUZHEN TU'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND SET ASIDE DEFAULT is continued to December 2, 2022 per the order of the Presiding Judge. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JONES MEMORIAL HOMES, INC. VS. MARK ANTHONY SMITH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Dismissing Action For Failure To Amend Complaint After Demurrer Was Sustained With Leave To Amend
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 16, 2022 line 3. DEFENDANT MARK SMITH'S MOTION FOR DISMISSING ACTION FOR FAILURE TO AMEND COMPLAINT AFTER DEMURRER WAS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND is continued to January 13, 2023. On August 30, 2022 the Court issued its tentative ruling on defendant's motion alerting the Plaintiff of the deadline to file their amended complaint. Plaintiff did not contest the tentative ruling and did not appear at the hearing to present an order for execution to enable the Plaintiff to timely comply with the Court's ruling. The clerks rejected Plaintiff's attempts to file an amended complaint several times in late-September alerting the Plaintiff that an order authorizing the filing of an amended complaint had to be on file. A month later, on October 20, 2022, Plaintiff submitted a proposed order for execution. Such proposed order, as drafted by the Plaintiff, would have directed the Plaintiff to comply with a deadline that had already passed. As of November 14, 2022 there is no motion for relief from August 31, 2022 deadline to file an amended complaint on file. Plaintiff to file a motion for relief and properly set it for hearing for January 13, 2023. Plaintiff shall address in the motion why a proposed order was not presented for execution at 9:30 a.m. on August 31, 2022 in light of August 31, 2022 at 5:00 p.m. deadline; why a proposed order had not been sent in until October 18, 2022; why no action was taken earlier in light of filing rejections (with reasons provided in the rejection notifications). Plaintiff to present a proposed order repeating this ruling verbatim for consideration at the hearing on November 15, 2022. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
